# dotapi

OpenDota API

- API version: 31.1.0

- Generator version: 7.14.0

# Introduction
The OpenDota API provides Dota 2 related data including advanced match data extracted from match replays.

You can find data that can be used to convert hero and ability IDs and other information provided by the API from the [dotaconstants](https://github.com/odota/dotaconstants) repository.

You can use the API without a key, but registering for a key allows increased rate limits and usage. Check out the [API page](https://www.opendota.com/api-keys) to learn more.
    


*Automatically generated by the [OpenAPI Generator](https://openapi-generator.tech)*

## Requirements

Building the API client library requires:

1. Java 1.8+
2. Maven/Gradle

## Installation

To install the API client library to your local Maven repository, simply execute:

```shell
mvn clean install
```

To deploy it to a remote Maven repository instead, configure the settings of the repository and execute:

```shell
mvn clean deploy
```

Refer to the [OSSRH Guide](http://central.sonatype.org/pages/ossrh-guide.html) for more information.

### Maven users

Add this dependency to your project's POM:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>ccom.ako.dbuff.dotapi</groupId>
  <artifactId>dotapi</artifactId>
  <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
  <scope>compile</scope>
</dependency>
```

### Gradle users

Add this dependency to your project's build file:

```groovy
  repositories {
    mavenCentral()     // Needed if the 'dotapi' jar has been published to maven central.
    mavenLocal()       // Needed if the 'dotapi' jar has been published to the local maven repo.
  }

  dependencies {
     implementation "ccom.ako.dbuff.dotapi:dotapi:0.0.1-SNAPSHOT"
  }
```

### Others

At first generate the JAR by executing:

```shell
mvn clean package
```

Then manually install the following JARs:

- `target/dotapi-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar`
- `target/lib/*.jar`

## Getting Started

Please follow the [installation](#installation) instruction and execute the following Java code:

```java

import com.ako.dbuff.dotapi.invoker.*;
import com.ako.dbuff.dotapi.invoker.auth.*;
import com.ako.dbuff.dotapi.model.*;
import com.ako.dbuff.dotapi.api.BenchmarksApi;

public class BenchmarksApiExample {

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        ApiClient defaultClient = Configuration.getDefaultApiClient();
        defaultClient.setBasePath("https://api.opendota.com/api");
        
        BenchmarksApi apiInstance = new BenchmarksApi(defaultClient);
        String heroId = "heroId_example"; // String | Hero ID
        try {
            BenchmarksResponse result = apiInstance.getBenchmarks(heroId);
            System.out.println(result);
        } catch (ApiException e) {
            System.err.println("Exception when calling BenchmarksApi#getBenchmarks");
            System.err.println("Status code: " + e.getCode());
            System.err.println("Reason: " + e.getResponseBody());
            System.err.println("Response headers: " + e.getResponseHeaders());
            e.printStackTrace();
        }
    }
}

```

## Documentation for Authorization


Authentication schemes defined for the API:
<a id="api_key"></a>
### api_key


- **Type**: API key
- **API key parameter name**: api_key
- **Location**: URL query string


## Recommendation

It's recommended to create an instance of `ApiClient` per thread in a multithreaded environment to avoid any potential issues.
